item.title: Germany Halves Growth Forecast for 2026 After Hit From Iran War
item.description: Germany cut its growth forecast for this year in half after the US war on Iran triggered a spike in energy prices for industry and households.

item.title: AllianceBernstein Shuts Hedge Fund Arya as Lack of Scale Bites
item.description: AllianceBernstein Holding LP has shut its hedge fund AB Arya, the latest sign of how a lack of scale is hindering challengers to the larger multi-strat firms.

item.title: US Futures Advance as President Trump Extends Truce With Iran
item.description: US stock-index futures advanced before the bell on Wednesday as traders examined the state of play for the Middle East following President Donald Trump indefinite ceasefire extension with Iran.

item.title: Dell’s XPS 16 Laptop Marks a Return to Form After PC Business Got ‘Off Course’
item.description: It’s not unheard of for tech companies to reverse a big marketing decision, but few have done so as quickly as Dell Technologies Inc. In early 2025, the company announced it would move away from its long-running XPS laptop branding in favor of simpler naming schemes like “Pro” and “Max” intended to appeal to a broader market of consumers accustomed to Apple Inc.’s naming strategy. By the following January, Dell realized it had made a mistake and quickly pivoted back.

item.title: $30 Billion Export Deluge Is Milei’s Chance to Rebuild Reserves
item.description: Argentina is beginning to receive a flood of hard currency that analysts see around $30 billion over the next six months, giving President Javier Milei his best chance to build up international reserves.

item.title: Google Cloud Releases New TPU Chip Lineup in Bid to Speed Up AI
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google Cloud division unveiled the latest generation of its tensor processing unit, or TPU, a homegrown chip that’s designed to make AI computing services faster and more efficient.

item.title: Google Launches $750 Million Fund for Consultants to Adopt AI
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google Cloud is launching a $750 million fund to help consulting firms including McKinsey & Co., Accenture Plc and Deloitte bring agentic artificial intelligence to their clients.

item.title: Iran War to Impact Shipping Long After It Ends, Japan’s MOL Says
item.description: The impact of the Middle East conflict on the shipping industry and global supply chains will linger long after the war ends, according to the head of one of the world’s largest tanker owners.

item.title: Hyundai, ST Engineering Bid for $530 Million Thai Frigate Order
item.description: South Korea’s Hyundai Heavy Industries Co. and Singapore’s ST Engineering Ltd. are among six companies vying for a $530 million contract to supply a high-capability frigate to Thailand, which is seeking to strengthen its maritime defenses.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 22, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York the morning after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: Barclays Spain Managing Director Martinez Leaves for Insurer
item.description: A 15-year veteran managing director for Barclays Plc in Spain has joined closely held insurer Santa Lucia SA to lead its strategy.

item.title: Italy’s Giorgetti Says Deficit to Be Under EU Limit in 2026
item.description: Premier Giorgia Meloni’s government insisted Italy can still bring its deficit down to below the European Union’s ceiling this year, even if the economy performs worse than forecast.

item.title: Coffee Climbs on Iran Conflict Concerns and Brazil Weather Risk
item.description: Arabica coffee climbed the most in two weeks in New York, amid concerns that the Middle East conflict will keep logistics costs elevated and as weather threatens Brazilian crops.

item.title: ECB’s Stournaras Says Central Bank ‘Should Wait’ on Rate Hike
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras said the central lender “should wait” ahead of a rate decision meeting next week.

item.title: DeepSeek in Talks to Raise at $20 Billion Value, Information Says
item.description: Chinese technology giants Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. are in discussions to invest funds into DeepSeek that would value the artificial intelligence startup above $20 billion, according to The Information.

item.title: Directors Quit Hotel Unit at Center of Singapore Billionaire Kwek Feud
item.description: Three directors have abruptly resigned from a hotel subsidiary controlled by one of Singapore’s wealthiest families, the latest twist in a corporate shakeup triggered by an internal feud.

item.title: Commodity Houses Are Perfectly Placed for a Wartime Trading Bonanza
item.description: Trading firms have put the lessons of the 2022 energy crisis to good use during the Iran war.

item.title: Europe Power Markets See More Negative Prices as Solar Surges
item.description: Europe’s power markets are seeing a sharp rise in negative electricity prices, particularly around midday when solar generation exceeds demand.

item.title: Philip Morris Smoke-Free Shift Gathers Pace as Profit Rises
item.description: Philip Morris International Inc.’s profit rose more than expected in the first quarter on sales of smoke-free products including heated tobacco and nicotine pouches.

item.title: Boeing Narrows Cash Burn in First Quarter as Deliveries Rise
item.description: Boeing Co. reported lower-than-expected cash outflow as it delivered the most aircraft in the first quarter since 2019, continuing its recovery with higher output and more steady operations at its defense and services units.

item.title: As Blue Owl’s Strains Worsened, Its Bankers Pitched a Long Shot
item.description: Blue Owl Capital, more than almost any other firm on Wall Street, needed a win.

item.title: Vista Strikes Deal to Speed Up Google AI in Software Portfolio
item.description: Vista Equity Partners is partnering with Alphabet Inc.’s Google Cloud to accelerate the deployment of artificial intelligence across the private equity firm’s portfolio of more than 90 software firms.

item.title: Wall Street’s Bullish Stocks View Contrasts With Consumer Gloom
item.description: Stocks are trading near a record high, signaling Wall Street is learning to cope with lingering geopolitical risks. Main Street is struggling to catch up.

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Says Too Early to Predict Next Week’s Rate Outcome
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Martin Kocher said uncertainty over the Iran war means he can’t yet predict what will happen when rate-setters meet in Frankfurt April 29-30.

item.title: Warsh Wants Fed to Back Bessent-Rubio ‘Statecraft’ Agenda for the Dollar
item.description: Asked what’s most important to support the dollar, Warsh answered “Deliver stable prices.”

item.title: Trump Weighs In on Companies’ Efforts to Recoup Tariff Payments
item.description: The US customs agency this week kicked off a effort to deliver tariff refunds online

item.title: Chip Bonus Boom: How Korea Learned Everyone Cares About Other People’s Money
item.description: Samsung workers press chipmaker to follow SK Hynix example and share massive profits

item.title: Oil Trader CFOs Say Hormuz Closure Driving Wave of Disputes
item.description: The chief financial officers of some of the world’s top commodity trading houses warned that the protracted closure of the Strait of Hormuz may lead to a wave of disputes stemming from lost supply.

item.title: Turkey Holds Rates as Iran War Clouds Inflation Outlook
item.description: Turkey’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate steady amid rising inflation expectations caused by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: US Home-Purchase Applications Jump as Rates Settle Back
item.description: US mortgage applications for home purchases increased last week by the most since early January as financing costs continued to ease, a tentative sign that the housing market is grinding forward.
